title: REST API Integration in React Native Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of fetch API, API client patterns, React Query, and file uploads in React Native.

questions.question: What does `response.ok` check in the fetch API, and why is it important?

options.text: It checks if the network connection is available
It returns true for HTTP status codes 200-299; important because fetch does NOT throw on 4xx/5xx errors — you must check it manually
It validates that the response body is valid JSON
It confirms the server received the request successfully

questions.question: In the centralized API client pattern, why should the 401 Unauthorized response be handled at the client level rather than in each individual API call?

options.text: The HTTP specification requires 401 to be handled at the transport layer
Token expiry applies to every request — handling it once in the client avoids duplicating logout logic across dozens of API calls
React Native does not allow access to response status codes in component code
Individual API call handlers cannot read authentication headers

questions.question: What does `staleTime: 5 * 60 * 1000` in React Query's useQuery configuration do?

options.text: Automatically refreshes data every 5 minutes in the background
Marks data as stale (triggering a background refetch) after 5 minutes have passed since last fetch
Cancels queries that take longer than 5 minutes to complete
Caches data for 5 minutes then permanently removes it from memory

questions.question: When uploading a file with fetch using FormData, why should you NOT set the 'Content-Type' header manually?

options.text: React Native does not support multipart/form-data uploads
Setting Content-Type manually overrides the automatically generated multipart boundary, breaking the server's ability to parse uploaded files
fetch ignores Content-Type headers when using FormData
You should always set Content-Type to 'application/octet-stream' for files

questions.question: What does `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['cart'] })` do in a React Query mutation's onSuccess callback?

options.text: Permanently deletes the cart data from the cache
Marks cart queries as stale and triggers an automatic background refetch to sync with the latest server state
Cancels any in-flight cart queries that are currently loading
Resets the cart to its initial empty state
